name: incident.io API
what_it_does: Modern incident management platform with Slack-native workflows, automated escalation, runbooks, and postmortem automation for engineering teams.
use_cases: Triggering incidents programmatically from monitoring alerts or agent-detected anomalies
Adding updates to active incidents via API for automated status page and Slack notifications
Querying incident history and timelines for postmortem generation by AI agents
Managing on-call schedules and escalation policies via API
Creating and updating custom fields on incidents to track AI-specific metadata
not_for: Teams without Slack (incident.io is deeply Slack-native)
Simple uptime monitoring without team coordination needs (use Statuspage)
Organizations needing on-premise deployment for compliance
best_when: Engineering teams using Slack who want modern incident management with strong automation APIs and good postmortem tooling.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use Slack or you need ITSM integration with ServiceNow/Jira Service Management as primary workflow.

auth.notes: API keys with scope selection (read, write, manage). Keys created per integration in dashboard. Bearer token auth header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 5 users with basic incident management
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth: ~$18/user/month; Enterprise: custom with on-call, analytics, SSO
pricing.notes: On-call scheduling requires paid tier. API access available on all tiers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear JSON errors with field-level validation messages; OpenAPI spec enables client generation
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident creation is not idempotent; query existing incidents before creating to avoid duplicates
known_agent_gotchas: Incidents require a severity and at least one role assignment before creation — missing required fields return 422
Webhook payloads include resource type and action — filter on incident_updated vs incident_created carefully
Custom fields must be pre-configured in dashboard before setting via API — dynamic field creation not supported
Incident status transitions follow defined workflow — cannot jump from triage to closed without intermediate states
API keys are tied to an actor identity in audit logs — create dedicated service account API keys, not personal ones

security_score_components.security_notes: Scoped API keys; webhook signature verification via HMAC. SOC2 Type II certified.
